role_title: Manager, Strategy & Operations, DoorDash for Business

level_signals: 6+ years of experience in strategy and operations, commercial management, operations, or consulting
Reporting to Sr. Manager of Strategy and Operations
Title is 'Manager' with significant cross-functional ownership
Base range $136K–$200K consistent with senior individual contributor or manager level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 6+ years of experience in strategy and operations, commercial management, operations, or consulting

You have a proven track record of owning and leading complex cross-functional workstreams, setting strategy, driving alignment across stakeholders, and consistently delivering measurable business outcomes in fast-paced environments.
You are a highly analytical and strategic thinker who can synthesize complex datasets into actionable recommendations, influence decision-making, and drive business results.
You are deeply customer-centric and able to balance the needs of customers, merchants, Dashers, and the business while building strong relationships and influencing stakeholders at all levels.

You thrive in ambiguity and are comfortable operating with limited direction. You proactively identify opportunities, challenge assumptions, develop strategic solutions, and drive execution from concept through implementation.

You are AI-forward and leverage AI, automation, and emerging technologies in novel ways to scale impact, improve team efficiency, accelerate decision-making, and solve business problems more effectively.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: direct management experience, high-growth startup (scaling a business from 0 - 1) experience is preferred
high-growth startup (scaling a business from 0 - 1) experience is preferred

scope.surface: DoorDash for Business — corporate meals and benefits platform; customer experience and defect reduction strategy
scope.business_model: B2B corporate benefits and meals platform on top of DoorDash marketplace
core_responsibilities: Proactively identify and solve root causes of customer friction to reduce defects and improve customer experience at scale
Analyze customer support and operational data to uncover critical trends and determine scope and impact of issues
Design solutions that prevent problems before they occur
Synthesize insights that highlight key pain points and advocate for the voice of the customer
Influence strategic decisions across Product, Operations, and Go-to-Market teams
Work with cross-functional partners to execute short and long-term plans
Deploy a data-driven approach to identify emerging customer support trends and develop solutions
Navigate ambiguity and bring structure to undefined opportunities
Create playbooks and templates and independently drive problems from identification through resolution

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($136K–$200K base) with equity mentioned
Clear mission and product surface described (DoorDash for Business corporate benefits)
Explicit expected fill date noted (8/18/26), indicating active and time-bound search
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Role has significant visibility across the business per JD
red_flags: Work location type not specified — no mention of remote, hybrid, or onsite requirements in the body; location type is unclear
Visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD
Salary range is wide ($64K spread), which may indicate uncertainty about level or location-based variation
SQL listed as 'strongly preferred' but framed in the nice-to-have section — could be a de facto hard requirement

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, title, and reporting structure)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
location.type (not stated in JD body; marked as unclear)
ambiguities: Work location is never specified in the JD body (no remote/hybrid/onsite language). The pay range references national base pay including Illinois and Colorado, and NYC legal notices are included, suggesting potentially remote-eligible or multi-location role, but this is not confirmed.

SQL is described as 'Advanced SQL experience strongly preferred' — language is preference but strength of wording ('strongly preferred') may make this a de facto hard requirement. Classified as nice-to-have per schema rules.
Direct management experience is listed alongside high-growth startup experience in the same sentence as 'preferred' — both treated as soft/nice-to-have.
